sentence: شْلَونَك؟ شَخْبَارَك الْيَوم يَا بُو صَالِح؟
raw: شلونك؟ شخبارك اليوم يا بو صالح؟
ipa: ˈʃloːnak ʃaxˈbaːrak alˈjoːm ˈjaː ˈbuː ˈsˤɑːliħ
gloss_en: How are you? What's your news today, Abu Salih?
notes: šaxbārak Kuwaiti greeting contraction (albalushi2016; QADI-KW); emphatic ص

sentence: اَلْيَوم غْبَار وَايِد وَالسِّمَا مُو صَافْيَة.
raw: اليوم غبار وايد والسما مو صافية.
ipa: alˈjoːm ˈɣbaːr ˈwaːjid ˈwassimaː ˈmuː ˈsˤɑːfja
gloss_en: Today there is a lot of dust and the sky is not clear.
notes: mū negator (Kuwaiti, albalushi2016); wāyid (Gulf); initial cluster ɣb-; sun assimilation as-s

sentence: مَا أَبِي قَهْوَة، أَبِي چَاي عَ الطَّرِيقَة الْكُوَيتِيَّة.
raw: ما أبي قهوة، أبي چاي ع الطريقة الكويتية.
ipa: ˈmaː ˈʔabiː ˈɡahwa ˈʔabiː ˈtʃaːj ˈʕa tˤtˤɑˈriːɡa lkuwajˈtijja
gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I want tea the Kuwaiti way.
notes: abī 'I want' (Kuwaiti); چ=/tʃ/ (alshammari2026); ق=/ɡ/ gahwa; ʕa-ṭ- article contraction

sentence: اِشْتَرَيت ثَلَاث أَكْيَاس عَيش مِن الْجَمْعِيَّة بِدِينَار.
raw: اشتريت ثلاث أكياس عيش من الجمعية بدينار.
ipa: ʔiʃtaˈreːt θaˈlaːθ ʔakˈjaːs ˈʕeːʃ ˈmin aldʒamˈʕijja bidiːˈnaːr
gloss_en: I bought three bags of rice from the co-op for a dinar.
notes: ʕēš 'rice' (Kuwaiti sense, albalushi2016); jamʕiyya co-op (Kuwaiti institution; QADI-KW); ث retained; ē

sentence: هَاذَا الرَّيَّال قَاعِد يِدَوِّر عَلَى شُغُل زَين.
raw: هاذا الريال قاعد يدور على شغل زين.
ipa: ˈhaːðaː rrajˈjaːl ˈɡaːʕid jiˈdawwir ˈʕalaː ˈʃuɣul ˈzeːn
gloss_en: This man is looking for a good job.
notes: gāʕid progressive (Gulf); rayyāl; ذ retained hāða; ق=/ɡ/ gāʕid; geminates يّ وّ; qaf→[ɡ] + interdental retention (Eastern Arabian) per (albalushi2016)

gloss_en: Get up, make the tea, and let's sit down.
notes: Kuwaiti imperatives gum/sawwi with qaf→g (alshammari2026); sun assimilation ash-shay

sentence: الْيَوم الجَو حَارّ وَايِد ومُو زَين.
raw: اليوم الجو حار وايد ومو زين.
ipa: alˈjoːm ˈaldʒoː ˈħaːrr ˈwaːjid ˈwmuː ˈzeːn
gloss_en: Today the weather is very hot and not nice.
notes: mū negator + wāyid intensifier (Kuwaiti; albalushi2016); jaww→[dʒoː]; ē/ō monophthongs

gloss_en: How much is this? Two dinars.
notes: bicham 'how much' with kaf-affrication چ=[tʃ] before /i/ (alshammari2026); dīnārēn dual; ذ retained

gloss_en: I have an appointment at five in the afternoon.
notes: mawʕid→[moːʕid] monophthong; sun assimilation as-sāʕa; emphatic ص; xams number; Eastern-Arabian vowel phonology + sun assimilation per (albalushi2016)

sentence: اِحْنَا سَاكْنِين فِي الجَهْرَاء مِن زَمَان.
raw: احنا ساكنين في الجهراء من زمان.
ipa: ˈʔiħnaː saːkˈniːn ˈfiː ldʒahˈraːʔ ˈmin zaˈmaːn
gloss_en: We have been living in Al-Jahra for a long time.
notes: iḥna 1pl pronoun (Gulf; albalushi2016); Jahra Kuwaiti town; jim=[dʒ]

gloss_en: Go straight, then turn right.
notes: sīda 'straight' + ʕugub 'then/after' with qaf→[ɡ] (Kuwaiti); liff imperative; geminate ff; qaf→[ɡ] per (albalushi2016)

sentence: أَكَلْت مَچْبُوس أَمْس وكَان زَين وَايِد.
raw: أكلت مچبوس أمس وكان زين وايد.
ipa: ʔaˈkalt matʃˈbuːs ˈʔams ˈwkaːn ˈzeːn ˈwaːjid
gloss_en: I ate machbous yesterday and it was very good.
notes: machbūs Kuwaiti rice dish; zēn 'good' + wāyid (albalushi2016); ē monophthong; respelled with چ — Kuwaiti machbuus has the affricate, matching the ar-QA row and the چ convention

gloss_en: I don't know which town he went to.
notes: adri 'I know' + dīra 'town/homeland' (Gulf lexicon; albalushi2016); mā negation

gloss_en: How many rooms are in the house?
notes: cham 'how many' with kaf-affrication چ=[tʃ] (alshammari2026); bēt→[beːt] monophthong; tā marbūṭa ġurfa

gloss_en: If you want to go, tell me.
notes: iḏa conditional + tabī 'you want' (Gulf); ذ retained iḏa; xabbir imperative; geminate bb; interdental ذ retention (Eastern Arabian) per (albalushi2016)

gloss_en: I went to the market and bought a new robe.
notes: riḥt 'I went', šarēt 'I bought' with -ēt suffix→[eː]; θōb retained ث; yidīd 'new' (jim→[j], Kuwaiti); qaf→[ɡ] sūg; sun assimilation as-sūg; qaf→[ɡ] and ǧīm→[j] lenition (Eastern Arabian) per (albalushi2016)

gloss_en: I will travel to Dubai in a week.
notes: Gulf phone/travel frame; retained long vowels and monophthongal [eː] (Dubai→[dibeː]); Eastern-Arabian vowel phonology per (albalushi2016)

gloss_en: I called you but you didn't answer.
notes: dagg 'to phone/knock' with qaf→[ɡ] (Kuwaiti); bas 'but/only'; -ēt suffix→[eː]; mā negation; geminates gg/dd; qaf→[ɡ] (dagg) per (albalushi2016)

gloss_en: Peace be upon you; how are you? (to a woman)
notes: shlōn 'how' + 2fs -ich kashkasha affricate [tʃ] (alshammari2026); sun assimilation as-salām; ʕalēkum

gloss_en: He who doesn't know the falcon grills it.
notes: Gulf proverb; sagr 'falcon' with qaf→[ɡ]; sun assimilation aṣ-ṣagr; mā negation; emphatic ص; qaf→[ɡ] (sagr) per (albalushi2016)
